text:
There once was a little girl named Lily who lived at the edge of a beautiful enchanted forest. Every morning, she would wake up and sing songs about goblins and glittering fruits. Her special tree next door had the softest leaves that danced like fairies. One day, while humming those cheerful tunes, Lily came upon an enchantingly old owl who hailed from a distant woodland realm.

The owl was peculiar. He had feathers painted sky blue, and his eyes sparkled like stars through the feathers. He was always talking about how many centuries he'd lived. Intrigued, Lily asked how many times he'd seen fireworks?

"Oh," he said dreamily. "Twelve hundred and fifty thousand. They make quite a show."

That night, under the moonlight, Lily dreamed of fireworks so bright they made stars fall. She woke feeling magical.

She decided every Christmas dawned with fireworks. Her little hamster, Mittens, would get excited, bouncing around.

Years went by,
